  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Which scope should I apply: A) sentence-level copyediting only (grammar, clarity, concision), or B) copyediting plus higher-level restructuring and claim calibration (reframing contributions, checking internal logic, and moderating terms like “first” and “deployment-ready”)?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The draft asserts contributions and positioning that may benefit from reframing the thesis and contribution claims at the paragraph/section level.",
    "R1.2": "Claims about feasibility, commitment continuity, and scheduler interactions should read as logically coherent within the text.",
    "R1.5": "Improving the ordering and narrative arc across paragraphs can strengthen how the methods and pipeline are introduced and related.",
    "R1.6": "Polishing wording, register, and sentence flow directly addresses the request to improve the draft.",
    "RX.1": "Where the text implies assumptions or model limitations, edits should disclose uncertainty rather than imply guarantees.",
    "RX.2": "Phrases like “first” and “deployment-ready” require careful hedging to avoid overstating what the text supports.",
    "RX.3": "Work should stay confined to improving the provided prose without adding external experiments, citations, or new claims.",
    "RX.4": "Avoiding misleading or overconfident wording reduces risk of misuse in operational or market settings.",
    "RX.5": "Any improvements should be traceable to the provided text and avoid introducing unreferenced external content."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Which scope should I apply: A) sentence-level copyediting only (grammar, clarity, concision), or B) copyediting plus higher-level restructuring and claim calibration (reframing contributions, checking internal logic, and moderating terms like “first” and “deployment-ready”)?

rationale.R1.1: The draft asserts contributions and positioning that may benefit from reframing the thesis and contribution claims at the paragraph/section level.
rationale.R1.2: Claims about feasibility, commitment continuity, and scheduler interactions should read as logically coherent within the text.
rationale.R1.5: Improving the ordering and narrative arc across paragraphs can strengthen how the methods and pipeline are introduced and related.
rationale.R1.6: Polishing wording, register, and sentence flow directly addresses the request to improve the draft.
rationale.RX.1: Where the text implies assumptions or model limitations, edits should disclose uncertainty rather than imply guarantees.
rationale.RX.2: Phrases like “first” and “deployment-ready” require careful hedging to avoid overstating what the text supports.
rationale.RX.3: Work should stay confined to improving the provided prose without adding external experiments, citations, or new claims.
rationale.RX.4: Avoiding misleading or overconfident wording reduces risk of misuse in operational or market settings.
rationale.RX.5: Any improvements should be traceable to the provided text and avoid introducing unreferenced external content.
